walkthrough.title: Unfold the Main Board
walkthrough.text: Let's start by unfolding the main board and placing it in the center of the table. This is the large board showing the World Capital with action spaces around the edges. Make sure everyone can reach it comfortably since you will all be placing exosuit-powered workers here throughout the game.

walkthrough.title: Place the Timeline Board
walkthrough.text: Let's set the timeline board next to the main board. This board tracks the current era and shows when the asteroid impact will hit. Place the impact tile face down on the designated impact space, which will be revealed later in the game.

walkthrough.title: Sort the Resources
walkthrough.text: Let's sort all the resource tokens into separate piles near the main board. You will need piles for water, food (wheat), titanium, gold, uranium, and neutronium. Also set out the energy core tokens separately since these are critical for powering your exosuits each era.

walkthrough.title: Prepare the Worker Pool
walkthrough.text: Let's organize all the worker tokens by type. There are four types: Scientists are the flask-shaped tokens, Engineers are the gear-shaped tokens, Administrators are the briefcase-shaped tokens, and Geniuses are the star-shaped wild tokens. Place them all in a general supply near the main board.

walkthrough.title: Shuffle the Building Stacks
walkthrough.text: Let's separate the building tiles into their three categories: mines, power plants, and factories. Shuffle each stack separately and place them face down near the main board. Reveal a few from each stack to form a building market that players can choose from during the game.

walkthrough.title: Set Up the Breakthrough Tiles
walkthrough.text: Let's shuffle all the breakthrough tiles and create a face-down stack. Reveal a row of available breakthroughs near the main board. These represent powerful technologies your faction can research during the game, and they score points at the end.

walkthrough.title: Prepare the Superproject Tiles
walkthrough.text: Let's find the superproject tiles and lay them out near the main board. These are the large, expensive constructions that score big points at the end of the game. Everyone should take a moment to read what each one requires so you can plan ahead.

walkthrough.title: Set Out the Paradox and Anomaly Tokens
walkthrough.text: Let's place the paradox tokens and anomaly tiles near the timeline board. You will receive paradox tokens if you fail to repay resources you borrowed from the future. If you collect too many paradox tokens, they convert into anomaly tiles, which clog up your board with permanent penalties.

walkthrough.title: Choose Factions
walkthrough.text: Let's have each player pick one of the four factions: Harmony, Dominance, Progress, or Salvation. Each faction has a unique player board with asymmetric abilities and a different starting configuration. Take your faction board, your faction-specific tokens, and read your special ability aloud so everyone knows what each faction does.

walkthrough.title: Take Your Starting Workers and Resources
walkthrough.text: Let's collect the starting workers and resources shown on your faction board. Each faction begins with a slightly different mix of workers and resources. Place your workers on the designated spaces of your faction board and your resources in your personal supply area.

walkthrough.title: Collect Your Exosuits
walkthrough.text: Let's grab your exosuit miniatures. Each player starts with a set of exosuits matching their faction color. These are required to place workers on the main board, and each one needs one energy core to power up each era. Place them on your faction board in the exosuit bay area.

walkthrough.title: Set Up the Evacuation Board
walkthrough.text: Let's place the evacuation board near the timeline board. This board becomes crucial after the asteroid impact, when you need to evacuate your people and assets to survive. The evacuation spaces show what you need to send and the victory points you will earn.

walkthrough.title: Place the Morale and Timeline Markers
walkthrough.text: Let's place each player's morale marker on the starting space of the morale track on the main board. Also place each player's timeline marker on the starting space of the timeline track. Both of these tracks affect scoring at the end of the game.

walkthrough.title: Determine First Player
walkthrough.text: Let's pick a starting player randomly and give them the first player marker. Turn order matters because main board action spaces are limited and contested. The first player marker will pass to the next player each era so everyone gets a chance to go first.

walkthrough.title: Review the Era Structure
walkthrough.text: Let's do a quick overview before we begin. Each era follows five phases in order: Paradox, Preparation, Warp, Action, and Clean-up. The game runs four to seven eras, with the asteroid impact hitting around the middle. After the impact, you shift focus to evacuation and final scoring.

Post-impact: focus shifts to evacuation + final scoring

walkthrough.title: Era Structure Overview
walkthrough.text: Let's walk through how each era works. Every era follows the same five phases in order: Paradox, Preparation, Warp, Action, and Clean-up. Understanding this flow is the key to playing Anachrony well.

walkthrough.title: Paradox Phase
walkthrough.text: Let's start with the Paradox phase, which happens at the beginning of each era. During this phase, check if any player has accumulated paradox tokens from unpaid time travel debts. If a player has too many paradox tokens, they must convert some into anomaly tiles, which take up building spaces on their faction board permanently.

walkthrough.title: Preparation Phase — Power Exosuits
walkthrough.text: Let's move to the Preparation phase, which has two parts. First, you decide how many exosuits to power up this era. Each exosuit requires one energy core to activate. Only powered exosuits can carry workers to the main board this era, so plan carefully based on your available energy cores.

walkthrough.title: Preparation Phase — Recruit Workers
walkthrough.text: Let's handle the second part of Preparation: recruiting new workers. You may recruit workers from the general supply based on the recruitment capacity shown on your faction board. Remember the four types: Scientists, Engineers, Administrators, and Geniuses. Geniuses are wild and can fill any worker requirement.

walkthrough.title: Warp Phase — Borrowing from the Future
walkthrough.text: Let's talk about time travel, the signature mechanic of Anachrony. During the Warp phase, you may request resources or workers from the future by placing warp tiles on your timeline. You receive these assets immediately, but you must repay them in a later era or face paradox penalties.

walkthrough.title: Warp Phase — Repayment Rules
walkthrough.text: Let's clarify how repayment works. When you borrow from the future, a warp tile goes on a future era slot on your timeline. When that era arrives, you must return the exact resources or workers you borrowed. If you cannot repay, you receive paradox tokens equal to the unmet debt.

walkthrough.title: Action Phase — Overview
walkthrough.text: Let's dive into the Action phase, where most of the game happens. Players take turns placing one worker at a time on action spaces. You can place on the main board, which requires an exosuit, or on your faction board, which does not require an exosuit. Play continues around the table until everyone passes.

walkthrough.title: Main Board Actions — Exosuit Requirement
walkthrough.text: Let's explain the main board in more detail. Every space on the main board requires you to send a worker inside an exosuit. The space also requires a specific worker type, so you need both the right worker and a powered exosuit. Some spaces have multiple slots, meaning multiple players can use them, while others are limited.

walkthrough.title: Action — Construct Buildings
walkthrough.text: Let's cover the Construct action on the main board. This lets you pay resources to build one of the available building tiles and place it on your faction board. Buildings provide ongoing benefits like extra worker slots, resource production, or end-game victory points. You need an Engineer in an exosuit to take this action.

walkthrough.title: Action — Recruit Workers
walkthrough.text: Let's look at the Recruit action on the main board. This action lets you take additional workers from the general supply beyond what you get in the Preparation phase. You need an Administrator in an exosuit. This is useful when you need specific worker types for upcoming actions.

walkthrough.title: Action — Research Breakthroughs
walkthrough.text: Let's explore the Research action on the main board. Spending resources here lets you claim one of the available breakthrough tiles, which provide powerful one-time or ongoing effects. You need a Scientist in an exosuit. Breakthroughs also score victory points at the end of the game.

walkthrough.title: Action — Mine Resources
walkthrough.text: Let's check out the Mine action. This lets you collect raw resources like titanium, gold, and uranium from the supply. You need an Engineer in an exosuit. The amount you receive depends on the specific mining space you choose, and some spaces give more than others.

walkthrough.title: Action — Purify Water
walkthrough.text: Let's look at the Purify Water action. Water is one of the most versatile resources in the game, used for many buildings and breakthroughs. You need a Scientist in an exosuit to take this action. The purification plant spaces provide water tokens from the general supply.

walkthrough.title: Action — Trade
walkthrough.text: Let's cover the Trade action. This lets you exchange resources for other resources at specified rates. You need an Administrator in an exosuit. Trading is essential when you have surplus of one resource but need something specific for a building or breakthrough.

walkthrough.title: Faction Board Actions
walkthrough.text: Let's talk about your faction board actions. These do not require exosuits, so they are available even when you run out of energy cores. Your faction board has spaces for generating resources from your buildings, activating special faction abilities, and other actions that vary by faction.

walkthrough.title: Clean-up Phase
walkthrough.text: Let's finish the era with the Clean-up phase. Return all workers and exosuits from action spaces back to your faction board. Advance the era marker one space on the timeline. Check if the asteroid impact has been revealed, and refresh the building and breakthrough markets for the next era.

walkthrough.title: The Asteroid Impact
walkthrough.text: Let's discuss the most dramatic event in the game: the asteroid impact. Around the middle of the game, the impact tile is revealed, changing the board state. Some main board action spaces become unavailable or altered. From this point forward, the evacuation board opens up and the endgame countdown begins.

walkthrough.title: Evacuation
walkthrough.text: Let's learn about evacuation. After the asteroid impact, you can spend actions and resources to evacuate workers, resources, and buildings through the evacuation board. Each successful evacuation earns victory points. Players who fail to evacuate effectively will fall behind in the final scoring.

walkthrough.title: Paradox Tokens and Anomalies
walkthrough.text: Let's revisit paradox in more detail. Every unpaid warp debt gives you paradox tokens. When you accumulate enough paradox tokens, you must take an anomaly tile. Anomalies occupy a building slot on your faction board permanently, blocking you from building there and costing you victory points at the end of the game.

walkthrough.title: Morale Track
walkthrough.text: Let's explain the morale track. Certain actions and events raise or lower your morale throughout the game. Higher morale gives you advantages during the game and bonus points at the end. Letting your morale drop too low can lock you out of certain benefits and cost you points.

walkthrough.title: Superprojects
walkthrough.text: Let's talk about superprojects, the crown jewels of Anachrony. These are massive constructions that require heavy resource investment but award large victory point bonuses. You build them using the Construct action, but they cost significantly more than regular buildings. Only one player can claim each superproject.

walkthrough.title: End of Game Trigger
walkthrough.text: Let's cover how the game ends. The game continues through post-impact eras until a set number of eras after impact have passed. Players have limited time to evacuate and complete their plans. Once the final era concludes, move to scoring.

walkthrough.title: Final Scoring
walkthrough.text: Let's count up victory points. You score for superprojects, breakthroughs, buildings constructed, successful evacuations, your position on the timeline track, morale bonuses, and remaining resources. Then subtract points for anomaly tiles. The player with the most victory points wins.

walkthrough.title: Tiebreaker
walkthrough.text: Let's handle ties if they happen. If two or more players are tied in victory points, the player with fewer anomaly tiles wins. If still tied, the player with more remaining resources wins. If that is still tied, you share the victory.

summary.title: Era Structure Overview
bullets: 5 phases/era: Paradox → Preparation → Warp → Action → Clean-up
Repeat for 4-7 eras total

walkthrough.title: Meet the Four Factions
walkthrough.text: Let's play through a practice game with four players. Player one is Harmony, player two is Dominance, player three is Progress, and player four is Salvation. Each faction has already taken their starting resources, workers, and exosuits as described in setup.

walkthrough.title: Era One — Preparation Phase
walkthrough.text: Let's begin era one. During Preparation, each player decides how many exosuits to power. Harmony powers three exosuits, spending three energy cores. Dominance and Progress each power two, and Salvation powers three. Everyone also recruits their free workers based on their faction board capacity.

walkthrough.title: Era One — Dominance Borrows Heavily
walkthrough.text: Let's move to the Warp phase. Dominance decides to borrow aggressively, requesting titanium and gold from the future for eras three and four. This gives Dominance a massive early resource advantage, but those debts will come due soon. The other players borrow modestly or skip the Warp phase entirely.

walkthrough.title: Era One — Action Phase Begins
walkthrough.text: Let's start placing workers. Harmony sends a Scientist in an exosuit to the Purify Water space, collecting two water. Progress sends an Engineer in an exosuit to the Mine space, picking up titanium. Each player takes one action at a time, going around the table.

walkthrough.title: Era One — Dominance Builds Early
walkthrough.text: Let's see what Dominance does with those borrowed resources. Dominance sends an Engineer in an exosuit to the Construct space and builds a power plant using the titanium and gold borrowed from the future. This gives Dominance early energy core production, a strong opening move.

walkthrough.title: Era One — Faction Board Actions
walkthrough.text: Let's watch Salvation use a faction board action. Since faction board actions do not require exosuits, Salvation places an Administrator on their faction board to activate a building ability. This is a great way to get value from workers when you have run out of powered exosuits.

walkthrough.title: Era One — Clean-up
walkthrough.text: Let's wrap up era one. All workers and exosuits return to faction boards. The era marker advances. Everyone checks their resource situation and starts planning for era two. So far, Dominance has the strongest board position but owes debts in eras three and four.

walkthrough.title: Era Two — Progress Runs Low on Energy Cores
walkthrough.text: Let's start era two Preparation. Progress realizes they only have one energy core left and can only power one exosuit this era. This is a problem because they need at least two main board actions to stay competitive. Progress made the mistake of not building or acquiring energy core production early enough.

walkthrough.title: Mistake One — Running Out of Energy Cores
walkthrough.text: Let's learn from Progress's mistake. Energy cores are the lifeblood of Anachrony because without them, your exosuits stay unpowered and you cannot access the main board. Always prioritize building a power plant or acquiring energy cores in the first two eras. Faction board actions alone cannot keep you competitive long-term.

walkthrough.title: Era Two — Warp and Actions
walkthrough.text: Let's continue era two. Harmony borrows one water from the future for era four, a small controlled debt. During actions, Salvation researches a breakthrough using a Scientist in an exosuit, gaining a powerful ongoing ability. Progress is limited to one main board action and one faction board action this era.

walkthrough.title: Era Three — Dominance's Debts Come Due
walkthrough.text: Let's enter era three and watch the Paradox phase closely. Dominance's first warp debt is due now: two titanium must be returned. Dominance checks their supply and realizes they spent all that titanium on buildings. They cannot repay the debt and receive two paradox tokens.

walkthrough.title: Mistake Two — Borrowing Without a Repayment Plan
walkthrough.text: Let's absorb this important lesson. Dominance borrowed heavily in era one without planning how to produce replacement resources by the repayment deadline. When you borrow from the future, always have a concrete plan to generate or acquire those exact resources before the due date. Unplanned borrowing leads to paradox spirals.

walkthrough.title: Era Three — Dominance Keeps Digging Deeper
walkthrough.text: Let's watch Dominance double down on a bad strategy. Instead of mining to prepare for era four's repayment, Dominance borrows even more from the future to build another building. This is a classic trap: using future resources to pay future debts, which only delays and compounds the problem.

walkthrough.title: Era Three — Building and Research Progress
walkthrough.text: Let's check on the other players. Harmony constructs a mine, securing steady titanium production. Salvation researches a second breakthrough, building toward a strong endgame score. Progress finally builds a power plant with their limited resources, solving the energy core crisis for future eras.

walkthrough.title: Era Four — The Asteroid Approaches
walkthrough.text: Let's start era four. The impact tile is revealed, showing the asteroid will hit at the end of this era. Players now know they have limited post-impact eras remaining. This is the signal to start thinking about evacuation plans and final scoring priorities.

walkthrough.title: Era Four — Dominance Faces More Paradox
walkthrough.text: Let's check the Paradox phase for era four. Dominance's second warp debt comes due: gold they borrowed back in era one. Once again, Dominance cannot pay. They receive more paradox tokens, pushing them over the threshold into an anomaly tile. This anomaly now permanently occupies a building slot on Dominance's faction board.

walkthrough.title: Era Four — The Asteroid Hits
walkthrough.text: Let's witness the asteroid impact. At the end of era four, the impact changes the board. Some main board spaces close or change. The evacuation board activates, and players must now balance between continuing to develop their factions and evacuating to score points.

walkthrough.title: Era Five — Post-Impact Scramble
walkthrough.text: Let's enter the first post-impact era. Harmony and Salvation immediately start evacuating workers and resources, spending actions on the evacuation board. Dominance is too focused on damage control from paradox to evacuate effectively. Progress catches up by powering three exosuits now that their power plant is running.

walkthrough.title: Era Five — Salvation Ignores Evacuation
walkthrough.text: Let's watch Salvation make a critical error. Despite the asteroid having hit, Salvation decides to spend all their actions on researching one more breakthrough and building a superproject, completely skipping evacuation this era. Salvation assumes they will have plenty of time to evacuate in later eras.

walkthrough.title: Mistake Three — No Evacuation Plan
walkthrough.text: Let's learn from Salvation's mistake. Post-impact eras are limited, and evacuation is a major source of victory points. Waiting too long means evacuation spaces fill up and you may not have enough actions to move everything. Start evacuating as soon as the impact hits, even if it means delaying other plans by one era.

walkthrough.title: Era Six — Final Push
walkthrough.text: Let's enter era six, the second post-impact era. Harmony completes a superproject and continues evacuating, locking in a strong position. Salvation scrambles to evacuate but finds many of the best evacuation spaces already claimed by Harmony and Progress. Dominance's anomaly tiles have severely limited their building options.

walkthrough.title: Final Scoring Breakdown
walkthrough.text: Let's tally the final scores. Harmony wins with strong marks in evacuation, buildings, and timeline position. Progress finishes second, having recovered from the early energy core crisis. Salvation places third, losing key points from the missed evacuation window despite having the most breakthroughs.

walkthrough.title: Review the Three Mistakes
walkthrough.text: Let's recap the three key mistakes we saw today. Progress ran out of energy cores by ignoring early power plant construction. Dominance borrowed from the future recklessly and spiraled into paradox and anomalies. Salvation delayed evacuation too long and missed crucial post-impact scoring opportunities.

walkthrough.title: Congratulations
walkthrough.text: Let's celebrate finishing your first practice game of Anachrony. You now understand the five-phase era structure, how time travel borrowing and repayment works, the importance of energy core management, and why evacuation timing matters. Ready to try a real game? Jump into a full session and put these lessons to work.

summary.title: Congratulations
bullets: Covered: era phases, time travel, energy cores, evacuation timing
Ready for a full game — put these lessons to work!

walkthrough.title: Prioritize Energy Core Production
walkthrough.text: Let's start with the most important tip in Anachrony. Build a power plant in the first two eras or you will be starved of exosuits for the rest of the game. Without powered exosuits, you cannot take main board actions, which are the strongest plays available to you.

walkthrough.title: Borrow Conservatively from the Future
walkthrough.text: Let's talk about responsible time travel. Only borrow resources when you have a clear plan to repay them before the deadline. A good rule of thumb is to never have more than two outstanding warp debts at once until you are comfortable with the game's economy.

walkthrough.title: Match Workers to Actions
walkthrough.text: Let's think about worker recruitment strategically. Recruit the worker types you actually need for your planned actions. If you are focused on building, recruit Engineers. If you are focused on research, recruit Scientists. Geniuses are flexible but scarce, so save them for turns when you need a type you are short on.

walkthrough.title: Watch the Impact Timer
walkthrough.text: Let's keep an eye on the asteroid. The impact is the most important event in the game and it dictates your strategic pivot. Start stockpiling resources for evacuation one to two eras before the impact hits. Players who are caught off guard lose critical tempo in the endgame.

walkthrough.title: Use Faction Board Actions Wisely
walkthrough.text: Let's not overlook your faction board. Faction board actions do not require exosuits, making them your fallback when energy cores run low. Build buildings that enhance your faction board output so you always have productive options even in lean eras.

walkthrough.title: Diversify Your Scoring Sources
walkthrough.text: Let's think about the endgame from the start. Do not put all your points into one basket. Aim for at least three strong scoring categories such as buildings plus evacuation plus breakthroughs. Players who hyper-focus on one area often get beaten by well-rounded opponents.

walkthrough.title: Pay Attention to Other Players' Exosuits
walkthrough.text: Let's talk about reading the table. Count how many exosuits your opponents have powered each era. This tells you how many main board actions they can take and helps you predict which spaces will be contested. Grab the spaces you need most before they fill up.

walkthrough.title: Lean into Your Faction's Strengths
walkthrough.text: Let's play to your faction's unique abilities. Each faction has asymmetric powers designed to support a particular strategy. Harmony excels at morale, Dominance at aggression, Progress at technology, and Salvation at long-term planning. Build around your faction's strengths rather than fighting against them.

summary.title: Prioritize Energy Core Production
bullets: Build power plant in eras 1-2
No cores = no exosuits = no main board access

summary.title: Borrow Conservatively from the Future
bullets: Only borrow with a clear repayment plan
Max 2 outstanding warp debts until comfortable

summary.title: Match Workers to Actions
bullets: Recruit worker types matching your strategy
Engineers for building, Scientists for research
Save Geniuses (wild) for gap-filling

summary.title: Watch the Impact Timer
bullets: Stockpile evac resources 1-2 eras before impact
Being caught off guard = lost endgame tempo

summary.title: Use Faction Board Actions Wisely
bullets: No exosuit required — fallback when cores are low
Build buildings that boost faction board output

summary.title: Diversify Your Scoring Sources
bullets: Aim for 3+ strong scoring categories
e.g., buildings + evacuation + breakthroughs
Hyper-focus loses to well-rounded play

summary.title: Pay Attention to Other Players' Exosuits
bullets: Count opponents' powered exosuits each era
Predict contested spaces → grab key spots early

summary.title: Lean into Your Faction's Strengths
bullets: Harmony → morale; Dominance → aggression
Progress → technology; Salvation → long-term planning
Build around asymmetric powers, not against them

walkthrough.title: Controlled Paradox Acceptance
walkthrough.text: Let's explore a bold advanced tactic. Sometimes accepting one or two paradox tokens deliberately is worth it if the borrowed resources let you build a superproject or complete a key breakthrough early. The point swing from the superproject must outweigh the paradox penalty, so do the math before committing.

walkthrough.title: Rush Superprojects
walkthrough.text: Let's talk about the superproject rush. Since only one player can claim each superproject, identifying and racing toward one early gives you a massive scoring advantage. Use early warp borrows specifically to fuel a superproject build in eras two or three, then repay the debt with your new production engine.

walkthrough.title: Exosuit Denial
walkthrough.text: Let's discuss a competitive edge move. In a tight game, count your opponents' energy cores and exosuits carefully. If you can claim contested main board spaces early in the action phase, you effectively waste their powered exosuits for the era. This is especially strong when an opponent only powered one or two exosuits.

walkthrough.title: Timeline Manipulation
walkthrough.text: Let's optimize your timeline position. The timeline track awards significant endgame points, and certain actions advance you further than others. Prioritize actions that push your timeline marker forward, especially when the marginal resource gain from an alternative action is small compared to the timeline bonus.

walkthrough.title: Evacuation Timing Optimization
walkthrough.text: Let's master the endgame evacuation. The best players evacuate in waves, starting immediately after impact with their most valuable assets. Evacuate high-point items first because if the game ends before you finish, you want the biggest payoffs already locked in. Leave cheap evacuations for the final era.

walkthrough.title: Genius Worker Economy
walkthrough.text: Let's unlock the power of Genius workers. Geniuses can fill any worker type requirement, making them the most flexible resource in the game. In the mid-to-late game, recruiting Geniuses lets you react to whatever main board spaces are available rather than being locked into specific action types by your worker roster.

walkthrough.title: Faction-Specific Power Spikes
walkthrough.text: Let's identify when each faction peaks. Harmony spikes when morale is high and they can leverage morale bonuses across multiple scoring categories. Dominance spikes in the early game with aggressive borrowing and building. Progress spikes when they chain breakthroughs together for compound effects. Salvation spikes in the late game with carefully timed long-range plans.

walkthrough.title: Late-Game Warp for Evacuation
walkthrough.text: Let's consider a risky but powerful late-game move. In the final era or two, borrow resources from the future specifically to fuel evacuation. Since the game ends before those debts come due, you effectively get free resources. The only cost is paradox tokens, so calculate whether the evacuation points exceed the paradox penalty.

summary.title: Controlled Paradox Acceptance
bullets: Deliberately take 1-2 paradox if the VP swing is worth it
Superproject VP must outweigh paradox penalty — do the math

summary.title: Rush Superprojects
bullets: Only 1 player per superproject — race to claim early
Use early warps to fuel era 2-3 superproject build
Repay debt with new production engine

summary.title: Exosuit Denial
bullets: Count opponents' cores + exosuits
Claim contested spaces early → waste their powered exosuits
Strongest vs. opponents with only 1-2 exosuits

summary.title: Timeline Manipulation
bullets: Timeline track = significant endgame VP
Prioritize actions that advance timeline marker
Compare marginal resource gain vs. timeline bonus

summary.title: Evacuation Timing Optimization
bullets: Evacuate in waves starting immediately post-impact
High-point assets first → lock in biggest payoffs
Cheap evacuations last (final era)

summary.title: Genius Worker Economy
bullets: Geniuses = wild workers → maximum flexibility
Mid-to-late game: recruit Geniuses to react to open spaces
Avoid being locked into specific action types

summary.title: Faction-Specific Power Spikes
bullets: Harmony: peaks w/ high morale → multi-category bonuses
Dominance: peaks early → aggressive borrow + build
Progress: peaks w/ breakthrough chains → compound effects
Salvation: peaks late → long-range plan payoffs

summary.title: Late-Game Warp for Evacuation
bullets: Borrow in final eras → debts never come due
Free resources for evacuation at cost of paradox only
Calculate: evacuation VP vs. paradox penalty

entries.definition: Worker type required for Research and Purify Water actions on the main board.
Worker type required for Construct and Mine actions on the main board.
Worker type required for Recruit and Trade actions on the main board.
Wild worker type that can substitute for any other worker type when placed on an action space.
Basic resource obtained through the Purify Water action. Used in many building and breakthrough costs.
Basic resource represented by wheat tokens. Used to sustain workers and pay certain costs.
Raw resource obtained through mining. Common building material for structures and superprojects.
Valuable resource obtained through mining. Used in advanced buildings and breakthroughs.
Rare resource obtained through mining. Required for high-tier buildings and superprojects.
The rarest resource in the game. Required for the most powerful constructions and breakthroughs.
Special resource required to power one exosuit for one era. Without energy cores, exosuits remain inactive.
Main board action that lets you pay resources to build a building tile on your faction board. Requires Engineer in exosuit.
Main board action that lets you take additional workers from the general supply. Requires Administrator in exosuit.
Main board action that lets you claim a breakthrough tile by paying resources. Requires Scientist in exosuit.
Main board action that lets you collect raw resources like titanium, gold, and uranium. Requires Engineer in exosuit.
Main board action that lets you collect water tokens from the supply. Requires Scientist in exosuit.
Main board action that lets you exchange resources at specified rates. Requires Administrator in exosuit.
Post-impact action that lets you send workers, resources, or buildings to safety for victory points.
Any action taken on your personal faction board. Does not require an exosuit to perform.
The act of borrowing resources or workers from a future era during the Warp phase. Creates a debt that must be repaid.
Token placed on a future era slot when you borrow from the future. Marks when repayment is due.
Returning the exact resources or workers you borrowed when the marked era arrives on the timeline.
Penalty token received when you fail to repay a warp debt. Accumulating too many leads to anomalies.
Permanent penalty tile that occupies a building slot on your faction board. Gained from excess paradox tokens. Costs victory points.
Track showing the progression of eras and warp debt slots. Also a scoring track for endgame victory points.
First phase of each era. Players check for unpaid warp debts and receive paradox tokens for failures.
Third phase of each era. Players may borrow resources or workers from the future.
Faction focused on morale and balanced development. Excels at maintaining high morale for scoring bonuses.
Aggressive faction that benefits from early expansion and assertive play. Strong early-game presence.
Technology-focused faction that chains breakthroughs for compound effects. Strongest in the mid-game.
Long-term planning faction that builds toward powerful late-game payoffs. Patient, strategic playstyle.
Building type that provides resource production on your faction board. Generates raw materials each era.
Building type that produces energy cores. Essential for powering exosuits in future eras.
Building type that provides processing or conversion abilities on your faction board.
Large, expensive building that awards significant victory points. Only one player can claim each superproject.
Technology tile gained through the Research action. Provides powerful effects and scores victory points.
Victory points earned by evacuating workers, resources, and buildings after the asteroid impact.
Victory points awarded based on your position on the timeline track at the end of the game.
Victory points awarded based on your position on the morale track at the end of the game.
Victory points subtracted for each anomaly tile on your faction board at end of game.
Mechanical suit required to place workers on the main board. Must be powered with one energy core each era.
One full round of play consisting of five phases: Paradox, Preparation, Warp, Action, and Clean-up.
Mid-game event that alters the main board, activates the evacuation board, and begins the endgame countdown.
Second phase of each era. Players power exosuits with energy cores and recruit new workers.
Fourth phase of each era. Players take turns placing workers on action spaces until all pass.
Fifth and final phase of each era. Return workers and exosuits, advance era marker, refresh markets.
Personal player board showing your faction's unique abilities, building slots, exosuit bay, and faction actions.
Central shared board showing the World Capital action spaces where exosuit-powered workers are placed.
Board that activates after the asteroid impact. Shows evacuation spaces with requirements and victory point rewards.
Track on the main board measuring each player's morale level. Affects in-game benefits and endgame scoring.
Shared pool of workers, resources, and tokens available to all players.
Token indicating turn order. Rotates to the next player each era.
